summary: Postinstall exfiltrates developer identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs unconditionally on `npm install` and harvests installer identity data: it reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml to extract the developer's git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email, then collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, current working directory, CI provider, Node version, platform/arch, and package version, and POSTs the bundled JSON payload to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ — a generic Google Cloud Run autogen URL unrelated to the package's stated homepage (openai-agents-js.guide) and not associated with any OpenAI infrastructure. In-file comments characterize this as anonymous telemetry, but the payload includes named identity fields (scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, username, hostname) that uniquely identify the developer. The package name and description impersonate the official @openai/agents SDK (which it also lists as a dependency), and the author domain `openai-agents-js.guide` is not an OpenAI-owned domain — developers searching for OpenAI Agents helpers are the targeted victim pool.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
